message:
fix(messaging): defensive unsubscribe, empty-replica width adoption in LagSketch and EventSketch

Three robustness improvements to messaging control plane CRDTs:

1. **Defensive unsubscribe (SublistCounts)**: unsubscribe() now only
   decrements when the pattern already has local state from a prior
   subscribe or inbound delta.  Unsubscribing a never-observed
   pattern is a no-op, preventing unbounded growth of zero-value
   entries from spurious or adversarial unsubscribe calls.

2. **LagSketch width adoption**: merge() and apply_delta() now adopt
   the incoming bucket_width when the local state is empty (fresh
   replica joining an established cluster).  Once data exists the
   width is locked in and mismatched peers are silently ignored as
   a deployment configuration error.  apply_delta returns true on
   mismatch to break anti-entropy retry loops.

3. **EventSketch width adoption**: Same empty-replica width adoption
   pattern applied to EventSketch's merge() and apply_delta().

Tests: lag_sketch_adopts_incoming_width_when_local_state_is_empty
and related coverage for EventSketch and SublistCounts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
